summary: Required (>=0.15.0) clone scanner used by `quality`: advisory code clone families plus the Markdown near-duplicate engine that replaced the bespoke document near-copy gate. The code path runs `nose query` (`nose scan` was removed in 0.13.3). The committed dup-ratchet/clone baselines are seeded on 0.15.0 (schema v6) and keyed by gate-computed offset/path-independent content fingerprints (slice 4), not nose family ids; the family SET nose groups is still scanner-version-scoped.

notes: Preferred arbitrary-machine path is the upstream release installer, which downloads a prebuilt binary.
Homebrew users may install with `brew install corca-ai/tap/nose`.
Use `NOSE_BIN` only as a maintainer-local override when testing a sibling checkout.
Rerun the upstream release installer to update release-managed installs.
If installed by Homebrew, update through `brew upgrade nose` instead.

detect.failure_hint: Install nose >=0.15.0; the quality `doc-duplicates` phase fails closed without it (run `charness tool doctor nose --json`).

when_missing: Doc near-duplicate review has no fallback: the bespoke difflib gate was removed, so a missing nose means the quality `doc-duplicates` phase fails closed.

Do not claim document near-duplicate or clone-family review succeeded without a healthy nose >=0.15.0 binary (the doc engine alone works on >=0.13.0, but the committed code-clone fingerprint baselines are seeded on 0.15.0).
when_version_mismatch: nose < 0.13.0 lacks the Markdown duplication engine and silently reports zero doc families; treat any pre-0.13.0 binary as missing for doc review.
nose < 0.13.3 still ships the deprecated `nose scan`; the code path now requires `nose query` (scan was removed in 0.13.3).

The dup-ratchet/advisory gate keys code newness on a gate-computed offset/path-independent content fingerprint (slice 4), not nose's family id — but the family SET nose groups is still scanner-version-scoped. A binary other than the 0.15.0 the baselines are seeded on can regroup families and drift the fingerprint set, so the gate surfaces a tool_version skew WARNING and may hard-block on the drift (it never auto-degrades on skew); re-seed both fingerprint baselines per nose version and update via `charness tool update nose`. (0.14.0 emitted schema v4 and closed Python/Go/Java/Swift/Rust/Ruby/TypeScript lowering gaps vs 0.13's schema v3; 0.15.0 emits schema v6.)
Treat changes in ranking, clone channels, or JSON output across versions as advisory drift before promoting findings into gates.

host_notes: `nose` is REQUIRED (>=0.15.0) in Charness quality: code clone families stay advisory (refactoring candidates), and the Markdown engine backs the doc near-duplicate review that replaced the bespoke difflib gate. Findings are advisory; tool absence fails closed.

Code clones: `nose query <root> all top=20 sort=extractability --mode syntax,semantic,near --min-size 24 --format json` per scope root (scripts, skills/public, skills/support), merged by `inventory_nose_clones.py`; 0.13.3 removed `nose scan` and `query` takes one path per call.

Doc near-duplicates: `nose query . --exclude 'plugins/**' --exclude 'charness-artifacts/**' --format json` and read the top-level `markdown` array (advisory `inventory_doc_duplicates.py` with its own drift baseline).
